question_text: A student applies a force to a door handle that is 0.8 m from the door's hinge. Another student applies the same force at a point only 0.2 m from the hinge. Which student will find it easier to open the door?
correct_answer: The student applying force 0.8 m from the hinge (the first student)
explanation: The further away from the pivot (hinge) a force is applied, the larger the moment produced. A larger moment makes it easier to turn or rotate an object, so the student at 0.8 m will have a bigger turning effect and find it easier to open the door.
